videos.title: Zach Osborne: From $175K to $2.5M in One Supercross Season
videos.description:
Supercross Champion Zach Osborne sits down to discuss his incredible motocross career—from MXGP World Championship success to Supercross dominance. But this isn't just racing talk. Zach breaks down the philosophy, mental discipline, and life decisions that shaped him after retirement.

🏆 THIS EPISODE COVERS:
• The $2.7M year that changed everything
• Meditation & ego: Why "avoiding ego IS ego"
• Why he retired at his peak (and has no regrets)
• The "monk mode" lifestyle choice that reset his mind
• Training with Ryan Dungey & Aldon Baker
• MXGP vs. Supercross: The reality
• Eli Tomac's speed & Jeffrey Herlings' obsession
• Building ClubMX from scratch
• Recovery from a broken back
• Life after professional motocross

⏱️ KEY TIMESTAMPS:
00:00 — Intro
01:50 — Welcome & Childhood Stories
25:31 — Amateur Career Deep Dive
42:17 — Training with Aldon Baker
55:04 — Ryan Dungey Stories
01:27:14 — Euro MXGP Era
02:02:09 — Geico Honda Chapter
02:32:50 — Path to Supercross Championship
02:43:13 — First Supercross Title
02:59:53 — The Financial Reality ($2.7M year)
03:06:17 — Move to 450 Class
03:14:32 — Broken Back Recovery
03:23:51 — Pro Motocross Champion
03:29:21 — Why He Retired
03:36:50 — Life After Racing
03:50:27 — Closing

💡 WHY THIS EPISODE MATTERS:
Zach doesn't just talk racing—he breaks down the psychology behind peak performance, the power of meditation and ego management, and why knowing when to walk away is as important as knowing when to push. Essential for anyone interested in elite performance, business strategy, or life balance.

videos.title: Key & Peele - Black Ice
videos.description:
Members of a local news team are at odds over warning viewers about the threat that ""black ice"" poses to their safety this winter.

About Key & Peele: 
Key & Peele showcases the fearless wit of stars Keegan-Michael Key and Jordan Peele as the duo takes on everything from "Gremlins 2" to systemic racism. With an array of sketches as wide-reaching as they are cringingly accurate, the pair has created a bevy of classic characters, including Wendell, the players of the East/West Bowl and President Obama's Anger Translator.

videos.title: Melrose Community Pulls Together After Devastating Downtown Fire
videos.description: A small town in central Minnesota is cleaning up after a fire destroyed much of its Main Street, Nina Moini reports (1:55). WCCO 4 News At 5 – Sept. 9, 2016
videos.transcript:
a small town in central Minnesota is
cleaning up after a fire destroyed much
of its Main Street this was the scene
yesterday around noon in Melrose as
firefighters from surrounding
communities responded to this fire
luckily no one was hurt today however
eight families no longer have a home and
the fire destroyed six businesses as
Nina Mo explains the State Fire Marshal
is working to figure out the cause as
people in the town are working to help
each other rebuild their lives it had a
lot of history to Melo so it's kind of a
it'd be a sad loss a 100-year-old
building that housed Lisa ilias's coffee
shop is one of three the fire tore
through just a lot of L and the smoke
was Heavy in the hallway so we actually
had a number of occupants trapped in the
building melrose's fire chief Jeremy
Kramer says 90 firefighters worked to
put out the fire for about 12 hours
rescuing several people from their
apartment Windows above the businesses
you know every smalltown USC is is
struggling to keep your storefronts um
you know your main street storefronts we
want to keep them open it's nice to see
them occupied as six business owners
work to figure out how much the damage
will cost and when they can rebuild
others in the community are stepping up
offering all they can to Aid families
who are out of a home I mean we've had
strollers brought in we have games we
have hygiene we've got clothes from
newborn adults local business Martinez
grocery store offered up space to house
donations one volunteer says all of this
appeared in just a couple of hours after
the fire it is very heartwarming it's
very over overwhelming
it's it's very
sincere from people there's a small tone
closeness and I think everybody wants to
get together and help and do what they
can Nina Mo wcco4 news authorities don't
suspect any foul play in this fire right
now uh 6 months ago there was a fire at
a church in Melrose and someone did
intentionally set that police have not
arrested anyone in that case

videos.title: Is Winter Over?
videos.transcript:
yep tomorrow is February first and
usually in New England it is freezing
outside this time of year we have piles
of snow but look around right now and it
looks and feels more like spring so
we're asking where is winter no question
this January has been unusually mild you
have a big block of wood right here we
should all knock on it this story there
is no significant snow in sight and Eric
I'm going to ask the question that might
touch off a riot is winter officially
over let me say no to that but it's kind
of on life support is what we're looking
at right now it has been one of the
warmest January's on record and from
what we're seeing the trend looking
forward it's kind of gonna continue into
February party to find snow then look
what we found
I'll winter wonderland on a Charlestown
Street but this snow is fake it's part
of a set for a Disney film shoot I guess
you have to come to Charlestown and come
to Pleasant Street to see the snow the
ground is bare no ice on the river and
joggers bicyclists they're out in force
January gave us a mere three inches of
snow
I like sunny days and I don't mind if
it's not cold there are at least four
meteorological forces at play that are
giving us this extremely mild winter the
big one is something we call the eastern
Pacific oscillation or EPO this pattern
keeps the cold bottled up in Alaska and
sends milder Pacific air into the lower
48 then there's the polar vortex it's
been strong which helps to corral the
coldest air around the North Pole now
you add in the Greenland block which
this year is a blocking that means that
storms that we do see tend to be fast
movers and more to the west favoring
more rain or mix than snow the last
factor is the Matt and Julian
oscillation the name it really rolls off
the tongue
this one describes thunderstorm activity
along the equator on the other side of
the world and this year it's not been
very active the only time it has been
it's been in a place that favors a
milder eastern US and for some the
unseasonable weather is a loss
[Music]
while others are counting their
blessings come the day that we have
terrible weather again we're all gonna
say oh I wish it was end of January when
it was beautiful clear blue skies so
take advantage of it
spot on tears but it's too warm outside
we say something's wrong with snowing we
say it's too snowy it's never just like
oh this is what we want right well you
just described all of those different
factors so what role if any does climate
change playing what we're experiencing
so we would say that in terms of when we
go forward milder winters that's gonna
be a trend already is a trend and that's
probably going to continue from the
temperature story it doesn't necessarily
mean less snow we've had less snowy
winters than this we're on a snowy roll
over the past 15 years so just because
the winters are getting warmer doesn't
necessarily mean we're be looking at
fewer snow storms or that we're not
going to see a big harsh winter again
like what we saw a few years ago all
right so let's talk about the weather
